output:
# MIRAGE and CONFER: Treating Imagined-Scene Action-Consistency as a Per-Augmentation Field instead of a Global Keep-All Constant in Semantic Robot Augmentation

## 1. Background and Current State of the Art

Data-efficient robot manipulation has converged on a dominant production recipe: behavior-clone a policy pi_theta on a handful of demonstrations, and manufacture visual generalization by SEMANTIC AUGMENTATION. A recorded demonstration d is segmented, and novel objects, poses, and backgrounds are inpainted or diffused to imagine many photorealistic scenes a, while the demonstrated action a_i^demo is reused unchanged as the supervision label on every imagined sample. This is the engine of the RoboAgent / MT-ACT line [1] and of its neighbors GenAug [2], ROSIE [3], and CACTI [4], all the way back to classic Copy-Paste of segmented instances [11, 12] using class-agnostic segmenters such as SAM [14] and latent-diffusion inpainting [15]. Empirically the recipe works: in the source paper, Copy-Paste-style semantic augmentation dominates spatial augmentation and no-augmentation, especially at unseen objects and unseen places, and the paper states outright that "generating semantically meaningful and physically plausible scenes is important." The whole value proposition is label reuse -- one demonstrated trajectory supervises hundreds of imagined variants -- and this is exactly what powers the line's plasticity / data-reusability story, in which a policy trained across 38 tasks is fine-tuned onto a new task (the toaster-oven protocol) [1].

The silent assumption underneath this reuse is that the recorded action STILL accomplishes the task in every imagined scene, i.e., action-consistency is a GLOBAL CONSTANT equal to 1 for all augmentations. But a generative augmenter can place a novel object off-pose, off-scale, non-graspable, or differently supported, so the reused gripper trajectory no longer makes correct contact or completes the task. Each such variant injects a MISLABELED state-action pair into the behavior-cloning (BC) buffer. Behavior cloning is precisely the regime where mislabeled targets are most corrosive: whether the policy is a low-data imitation learner like BC-Z [10] or a scaled transformer like RT-1 [9], BC has no corrective feedback loop, so label noise compounds into deployment-time action error [20]. The pipeline already HAS every signal needed to detect this -- segmentation, geometry, multi-view frames, and the policy's own predictions a_i^pred -- yet it measures nothing about per-augmentation action validity and feeds nothing back into the per-sample BC loss or label.

This is the opening this work targets: a measured-but-not-fed-back gap between a rich set of available validity signals on one side and an unchanged keep-all / hard-label augmentation rule on the other. Positioned against prior art, the semantic-augmentation lines [1, 2, 3, 4] never measure or use a per-augmentation action-consistency field; classic visual augmentation [11, 12, 13] assumes label-preservation by construction (valid for a perception label, not guaranteed for an action label); data-selection and soft-label lines [16, 17, 18, 21] developed single, separate knobs on generic label noise; and grasp/physics validators [23] act as hard external gates rather than calibrated, free, in-loss fields. Nothing connects the available validity signals to the BC objective, and that disconnect is what we name and close.

## 2. Key Findings and Analysis

We name the gap MIRAGE (Mislabeled Imagined-scene action Reuse under Assumed Global consistEncy). Semantic augmentation defines a labeling rule that copies a_i^demo onto every imagined scene a_i, implicitly asserting that the action-consistency s(d -> a) == 1 for all (d, a). MIRAGE states that action-consistency is in truth a HETEROGENEOUS per-(demonstration, augmentation) FIELD s: (D x Aug) -> [0,1], giving the probability that the reused action a_i^demo still correctly accomplishes the task in the imagined scene a_i. Formally, the deployed objective L_base = sum_i ell(pi_theta(o_i), a_i^demo) is merely the s == 1 special case of a consistency-weighted objective; whenever the realized field has s_i < 1 for some i, L_base optimizes against mislabeled targets.

The defining property of MIRAGE is that the harm is SILENT under pooled success rate: a success rate is an average over many evaluations, so a minority of mislabeled augmentations barely moves the mean (metric blindness). The harm does not spread uniformly -- it CONCENTRATES at unseen objects, unseen places, and high augmentation strength, exactly the subpopulations the source paper reports as its headline weakness ("not able to achieve similar performance as ours at unseen objects" [1]). And semantic augmentation is a MULTIPLIER: one bad demonstration is copied into hundreds of imagined-but-invalid samples (the source protocol fans a handful of demos into roughly 50 trajectories x 4 augmentations/frame = 250 augmented samples per scene [1]), and the line's reuse-across-tasks plasticity compounds it further. The harm VANISHES if and only if the field is constant, s_i == 1 for all i: when the augmenter only ever produces action-consistent scenes, MIRAGE disappears and L_base is already optimal. The gap exists exactly because the field is non-constant.

We introduce CONFER (CONsistency-Field Estimation and Reweighting), which promotes the action-consistency scalar to a field and acts on it with two orthogonal knobs. The field is estimated FOR FREE (or, where noted, at low overhead) from signals the system already produces: (e1) policy self-agreement -- the closeness of a_i^pred to a_i^demo on the augmented frame o_i, computed with a circularity-safe teacher (a frozen snapshot or cross-fit policy), a genuine byproduct of a forward pass; (e2) an affordance / contact / grasp-pose preservation proxy from the segmentation and geometry the augmenter runs -- this is low-overhead and reuses computation when the augmenter already runs geometry/contact checks (as in SAM-based pipelines [14]), while for inpaint-only augmenters e2 adds a lightweight grasp-pose analysis step; and (e3) multi-view consistency across the MT-ACT multi-view frames, a genuine free byproduct read from frames already captured. The raw estimate s_hat_i is passed through a small held-out CALIBRATION map c(.) -- fit by isotonic or Platt scaling on a PROBE set where action-consistency IS labeled (sim-rollout success or a VLM/human judge) -- yielding s_cal_i = c(s_hat_i), anchored so that c(1) = 1. KNOB1 (selection / gating / routing): g_i = 1[s_cal_i >= gamma] decides which imagined scenes enter the training buffer. KNOB2 (reweighting / soft-relabeling): the per-sample weight w_i = phi(s_cal_i) down-weights borderline augmentations (phi monotone non-decreasing, phi(1) = 1), and the soft-relabeled target tilde_a_i = lambda(s_cal_i) * a_i^demo + (1 - lambda(s_cal_i)) * a_i^pred softens toward the policy's own prediction when consistency is low (lambda monotone non-decreasing, lambda(1) = 1, default conservative). The CONFER objective is:

  L_CONFER = sum_i  g_i * w_i * ell( pi_theta(o_i), tilde_a_i ).

The decisive design property is the EXACT-COLLAPSE guarantee [provable-by-construction]. The collapse is a DESIGN SWITCH that disables estimation, not a claim that the true field equals 1: set s_hat_i = 1 for all i (disable estimation), so s_cal_i = c(s_hat_i) = c(1) = 1, hence g_i = 1[1 >= gamma] = 1 for any gamma <= 1, w_i = phi(1) = 1, lambda(1) = 1, and tilde_a_i = a_i^demo. Term-by-term, g_i * w_i * ell(pi_theta(o_i), tilde_a_i) = ell(pi_theta(o_i), a_i^demo), so L_CONFER == L_base sample-for-sample, with per-sample discrepancy E_collapse = max_i | g_i w_i ell(pi_theta(o_i), tilde_a_i) - ell(pi_theta(o_i), a_i^demo) | <= 1e-6 enforced by a CI-gateable build check (E_collapse <= 1e-6 is a finite-precision implementation budget, not a performance claim). This is a strict, safe generalization: zero regression at the identity default.

The novelty wedge is four clauses that, taken together, no prior line covers more than two of: C1 -- action-label consistency of an augmentation is modeled as a per-(demonstration, augmentation) FIELD s(d -> a), not a global keep-all constant (scalar -> field promotion); C2 -- the field is estimated for free / low-overhead from signals the augmenter and policy already produce (self-agreement + affordance/geometry proxy + multi-view), with NO extra human action labels at train time, passed through a small held-out calibration map c(.); C3 -- TWO orthogonal knobs act on the SAME calibrated field, a selection gate (gamma) and a soft-relabel reweighting (phi, lambda); C4 -- an EXACT-COLLAPSE strict generalization with a CI-gateable E_collapse <= 1e-6 back to vanilla keep-all hard-label augmentation (zero regression at the identity default).

BOUNDARY RECOVERIES (named baseline => exact special point of the CONFER field) [provable-by-construction]:

  R1 No-Aug (real demos only): gamma > max_i s_cal_i over augmented samples => g_i = 0 for all augmentations (gate rejects all).
  R2 Vanilla keep-all semantic aug (CopyPaste / MT-AUG; GenAug / ROSIE / CACTI hard-label reuse): s_i == 1 (constant) => g_i = 1, w_i = 1, tilde_a_i = a_i^demo => L_CONFER == L_base. This is the deployed baseline and the collapse target.
  R3 Pure data-selection / curriculum (hard filter): KNOB2 off (phi == 1, lambda == 1) => objective = sum_i g_i * ell(., a_i^demo) (threshold selection).
  R4 Soft-label / consistency- or uncertainty-weighted BC: KNOB1 off (gamma = 0 => g_i = 1 for all i); only w_i = phi(s_cal_i) and tilde_a_i act (reweight-only).
  R5 Oracle filter (sim/VLM-labeled validity): s_hat replaced by the true labeled consistency => upper-bound ceiling of the field.

Load-bearing symbols: d demonstration; a augmentation; o_i observation of augmented sample i; a_i^demo reused demonstrated action label; a_i^pred current-policy predicted action on o_i; pi_theta the BC policy; ell per-sample BC loss; s(d -> a) = s_i action-consistency field in [0,1]; s_hat_i free estimate; c(.) calibration map; s_cal_i = c(s_hat_i); gamma gate threshold; g_i gate indicator; phi reweight map; w_i loss weight; lambda soft-relabel mixing coefficient; tilde_a_i soft-relabeled target; L_base deployed keep-all objective; L_CONFER the CONFER objective; E_collapse exact-collapse budget; epsilon do-no-harm margin; keep_rate fraction of augmentations kept; identity default = disable estimation (s_hat_i = 1 for all i).

## 3. Novel Research Idea (5-Question Formulation)

**[Question 1] - What is the problem?**

Does promoting the reused action-label's consistency from a global keep-all constant to a per-(demonstration, augmentation) field s(d -> a) -- estimated for free from the policy's own self-agreement and affordance/geometry proxies, calibrated on a small probe, and used to gate and soft-relabel behavior-cloning targets -- improve generalization to unseen objects and places without regressing at the identity default, relative to vanilla keep-all semantic augmentation?

**[Question 2] - Why is it interesting and important?**

P1 (the status quo it disrupts: a global keep-all / hard-label policy). The dominant recipe for data-efficient robot manipulation behavior-clones pi_theta on a few demonstrations and manufactures visual generalization by semantic augmentation, reusing a_i^demo unchanged as the label on every imagined scene. This is the engine of the RoboAgent / MT-ACT line [1] and its neighbors GenAug [2], ROSIE [3], CACTI [4], back to classic Copy-Paste [11, 12]. That reuse silently asserts a GLOBAL CONSTANT, s(d -> a) == 1 for every imagined scene. CONFER disrupts exactly this unexamined constant: MIRAGE asserts s is a heterogeneous per-(demo, augmentation) field, and the deployed objective L_base is merely the s == 1 special case of a consistency-weighted objective. The community has been optimizing a corner of a richer objective without ever naming the corner.

P2 (the silent harm: mislabeled state-action pairs, multiplied, concentrated, invisible). A generative augmenter can place a novel object off-pose, off-scale, non-graspable, or differently supported, so the reused trajectory no longer makes correct contact -- injecting a mislabeled state-action pair into the BC buffer. This is a first-order data-quality failure, and BC is where mislabeled targets are most corrosive: with no corrective feedback loop, label noise compounds into deployment-time action error [20] for both low-data learners [10] and scaled transformers [9]. Semantic augmentation is a MULTIPLIER -- one bad demo becomes hundreds of imagined-but-invalid samples (about 50 trajectories x 4 augmentations/frame = 250 per scene [1]). The harm CONCENTRATES at unseen objects, unseen places, and high augmentation strength -- the field's headline weakness [1] -- and is INVISIBLE under pooled success (metric blindness). The pipeline already has every detection signal (segmentation, geometry, multi-view frames, a_i^pred) yet feeds none of it back: the measured-but-not-fed-back gap.

P3 (why it advances knowledge: an unmeasured assumption becomes a calibrated, inspectable field). The contribution is epistemic before it is empirical. CONFER turns the unmeasured assumption (s == 1 everywhere) into a calibrated, inspectable per-augmentation quantity s_cal_i = c(s_hat_i), estimated from e1 self-agreement, e2 affordance/contact/grasp-pose proxy, and e3 multi-view consistency, then passed through a small probe calibration c(.). This reframes the community's question from "which augmenter is best?" (a tournament among GenAug, ROSIE, CACTI, Copy-Paste) to "WHERE is each imagined scene action-valid?" -- a per-sample, stratifiable question. It yields a reusable VALIDITY-PROBE protocol (AUROC / ECE of s against an oracle [19]) that any future augmenter can be audited with, independent of the policy it feeds. Naming s and measuring it is the durable knowledge; the two knobs are the first thing one does once the field exists.

P4 (practical payoff: low cost, augmenter-agnostic, drop-in, risk-free adoption). The estimator signals are cheap: a_i^pred is a byproduct of a forward pass (e1) and multi-view consistency reads frames already captured (e3), so these are genuinely free; the affordance/geometry proxy (e2) is low-overhead, reusing computation when the augmenter already runs geometry/contact checks (as in SAM-based pipelines [14]) and adding only a lightweight grasp-pose analysis step for inpaint-only augmenters. CONFER is therefore augmenter-agnostic and backbone-agnostic (MT-ACT / ACT [7], TransporterNet [5], CLIPort [6], Diffusion Policy [8]) and drops into an existing pipeline as a per-sample gate g_i and weight w_i. Adoption is risk-free by construction: disabling estimation (set s_hat_i = 1 for all i, so g_i = 1, w_i = 1, lambda(1) = 1, tilde_a_i = a_i^demo) reproduces vanilla keep-all augmentation sample-for-sample with E_collapse <= 1e-6, removing the usual barrier to touching a working pipeline; a do-no-harm non-inferiority margin epsilon underwrites the near-identity regime.

P5 (the stakes: plasticity / data-reuse compounds the harm; deployment and low-demo regimes raise the cost). The RoboAgent story is a PLASTICITY and DATA-REUSE story -- a policy trained on 38 tasks is fine-tuned onto a new task [1], and reuse is the whole point. But reuse COMPOUNDS MIRAGE: a possibly-invalid reused label is propagated not just across imagined scenes but across tasks, so one mislabeled augmentation can corrupt many downstream transfers. CONFER protects reuse precisely where the field leans hardest on it. The downstream stakes are concrete: in safe real-robot deployment a policy trained on invalid grasp targets can drive unsafe contacts, and in low-demo regimes (5-50 demos) each mislabeled multiplier is a larger fraction of the effective supervision, so sample efficiency degrades fastest exactly where augmentation is supposed to help most. Closing MIRAGE targets the dominant scaling axis of the line, at the metric the community cares about, for free, with a provable safety net.

**[Question 3] - Why is it hard?**

O1 CIRCULARITY [REPRESENTATIONAL]. One free estimator channel is the policy judging its own augmentations (e1 self-agreement: closeness of a_i^pred to a_i^demo). NAIVE FAILS: a policy already invariant to an edit emits a_i^pred ~ a_i^demo and scores the scene "consistent" even when the reused action is wrong, so s_hat_i drifts toward trivial agreement and the gate keeps exactly the mislabeled samples MIRAGE wants to catch; you cannot tune gamma out of a contaminated signal. REPRESENTATIONAL because the fix is a structural choice about WHERE the scoring signal comes from. MITIGATION: anchor s_hat_i on non-policy channels (e2, e3); score the e1 term with a circularity-safe teacher (a frozen snapshot or a cross-fit policy trained on a disjoint demo/aug fold), never the live theta; calibrate against an external probe. Falsified by the PLACEBO rung; bounded by the ORACLE rung.

O2 CALIBRATION WITHOUT PER-AUGMENTATION LABELS [REPRESENTATIONAL]. KNOB2 and the gamma threshold need s_cal_i to behave like Pr[ reused action is valid in scene a_i ], but at train time there is NO action label for an imagined scene. NAIVE FAILS: raw policy confidence is miscalibrated and overconfident, so thresholding it conflates "the policy is sure" with "the action is valid." REPRESENTATIONAL because it forces the calibration map c(.) into the architecture -- s_hat_i is never used directly; only s_cal_i = c(s_hat_i) drives g_i, w_i, lambda. MITIGATION: fit c(.) on a small held-out probe where action-consistency IS labeled (sim-rollout success or a VLM/human judge) via isotonic / Platt scaling; anchor c(1) = 1 so EXACT-COLLAPSE holds; report ECE(s_cal) far below ECE(raw s_hat). The LEAKAGE rung keeps the probe split disjoint from eval and frozen.

O3 CONFOUNDING KEEP-RATE WITH VALIDITY [REPRESENTATIONAL]. KNOB1 gating changes dataset SIZE and DIVERSITY, and size alone changes BC generalization. NAIVE FAILS: a "we filtered and improved" result cannot distinguish gains from MEASURING consistency (the claim) from gains from merely training on less / different data (a confound). REPRESENTATIONAL because it forces a control INTO the design -- keep_rate becomes a first-class reported quantity and the placebo is built to match it. MITIGATION: a rate-matched consistent-shuffle PLACEBO replaces s_cal by a random field at the SAME keep_rate; report keep_rate with every success number; PASS requires CONFER > placebo at matched keep_rate by more than seed noise.

O4 SIM-TO-REAL OF THE PROBE / VALIDITY ESTIMATOR. The probe (and the e2 proxy) is cheap in simulation, but deployment is on a real robot. NAIVE FAILS: a c(.) fit purely on sim rollouts can encode sim-specific contact geometry and physics, so s_cal_i mis-ranks real augmentations. MITIGATION: validate the probe-fit c(.) on a small REAL labeled subset; report calibration TRANSFER (AUROC/ECE of s_cal on real vs sim labels); where transfer degrades, refit only c(.) on the real subset (cheap -- s_hat is unchanged). e2 reuses augmenter geometry, so it ports more directly than a learned sim-only validator.

O5 MULTI-VIEW / PARTIAL OBSERVABILITY. e3 relies on the MT-ACT multi-view frames, but the source protocol obstructs 1/2/3 cameras. NAIVE FAILS: a fixed-weight average treats an obstructed/blank view as informative, so e3 reports spurious (dis)agreement exactly when observability is worst. MITIGATION: per-view reliability weighting (down-weight/drop views detected as obstructed), and graceful fallback to e1 + e2 when views drop so the field never goes blind; test under the paper's camera-obstruction protocol and report s_cal quality as views are removed.

O6 METRIC BLINDNESS [REPRESENTATIONAL]. Pooled success averages over many evals; a minority of mislabeled augmentations barely moves the mean. NAIVE FAILS: reporting only the pooled mean HIDES both the harm (MIRAGE) and the fix (CONFER), which concentrate in the unseen-object/unseen-place tail and at high strength. REPRESENTATIONAL because it forces the EVALUATION's structure -- the primary readouts must be the stratified cells, since the method's value is the GRADIENT of lift across strata. MITIGATION: stratified success as the PRIMARY readout, cells = {Unseen Env, Unseen place, Unseen pick} x {augmentation strength low/med/high} at 1/10/100 demos; pooled success reported only for non-inferiority.

O7 SOFT-RELABEL TARGET DRIFT [auxiliary]. KNOB2's relabel face moves the target toward a_i^pred via tilde_a_i = lambda(s_cal_i) * a_i^demo + (1 - lambda(s_cal_i)) * a_i^pred. NAIVE FAILS: when consistency is low AND the policy is itself wrong, a_i^pred is a bad teacher; aggressive relabeling (lambda small) replaces one wrong target with another -- a second-order circularity (cousin of O1). MITIGATION: default lambda conservative (stay close to a_i^demo unless s_cal_i is high), couple relabel to the same circularity-safe teacher used for e1, and protect everything with the DO-NO-HARM gate; because lambda(1) = 1, relabel vanishes at the identity default and cannot regress keep-all by construction.

WHY IT IS NONETHELESS TRACTABLE. Every obstacle is matched by a structural feature already in the frame: circularity (O1) and target drift (O7) are fenced by non-policy channels (e2, e3) plus a frozen/cross-fit teacher; the missing-label problem (O2) is solved by a tiny calibrated probe rather than per-augmentation labels; the keep-rate confound (O3) and metric blindness (O6) are neutralized by the rate-matched PLACEBO and the mandated stratified readout; sim-to-real (O4) and partial observability (O5) degrade gracefully because the field is multi-channel and c(.) is cheap to refit. Above all, the EXACT-COLLAPSE identity (set s_hat_i = 1 for all i => L_CONFER == L_base, E_collapse <= 1e-6) guarantees CONFER does no harm relative to the deployed keep-all baseline -- the downside is pinned to zero by construction, so the entire research risk is on the UPSIDE.

**[Question 4] - Why hasn't it been solved before?**

Prior art falls into five families; for each we give representative work, what it does, the precise delta CONFER adds, and the boundary recovery (R2-R5) it maps to.

(1) Semantic / generative robot augmentation -- GenAug [2], ROSIE [3], CACTI [4], RoboAgent / MT-ACT [1]. WHAT: imagine novel scenes via inpainting/diffusion [15] (often gated by class-agnostic segmentation [14]) and REUSE a_i^demo unchanged (keep-all, hard label). DELTA: none measures or uses a per-augmentation action-consistency field; they assert s(d -> a) == 1 globally. CONFER adds the full wedge C1 (scalar -> field), C2 (free estimator + calibration c(.)), C3 (two orthogonal knobs on the SAME field), C4 (exact collapse). These are precisely R2: s_i == 1 => g_i = 1, w_i = 1, tilde_a_i = a_i^demo => L_CONFER == L_base.

(2) Classic visual data augmentation -- Copy-Paste / Ghiasi et al. [11], LVIS / Gupta et al. [12], domain randomization / Tobin et al. [13]. WHAT: inject invariance by pasting segmented instances [11, 12] or randomizing textures/lighting/poses [13], assuming LABEL-PRESERVATION by construction. DELTA: label-preservation is valid for a PERCEPTION label (a pasted cat is still a cat) but NOT for an ACTION label (a pasted object may not be graspable at the demonstrated contact). CONFER drops the action-label-preservation assumption and ESTIMATES it as s(d -> a). Used as hard-label augmenters these map to R2; CONFER is the strict generalization that re-validates the action target the perception-era methods took for granted.

(3) Data selection / valuation / curriculum -- influence functions / Koh and Liang [16], Data Shapley / Ghorbani and Zou [17], curriculum learning [22]. WHAT: score samples by value, influence, or difficulty and select/order them. DELTA: these are PURE-GATE, single-knob mechanisms -- no soft-relabel, no exact-collapse identity, and none is specialized to imagined-scene ACTION validity. They map to R3 (KNOB2 off: phi == 1, lambda == 1 => sum_i g_i * ell(., a_i^demo)). CONFER subsumes the gate as KNOB1 and adds the orthogonal relabel knob plus the calibrated, augmentation-specific field.

(4) Soft-label / consistency / uncertainty-weighted BC -- label smoothing / Szegedy et al. [18], consistency regularization / FixMatch [21], calibration / Guo et al. [19]. WHAT: soften or reweight targets by confidence or agreement under perturbation. DELTA: these are PURE-REWEIGHT, single-knob mechanisms -- no selection GATE, not targeted at augmentation-INDUCED label noise, and not calibrated against an explicit action-consistency PROBE. They map to R4 (KNOB1 off: gamma = 0 => g_i = 1 for all i; only w_i and tilde_a_i act). CONFER folds reweight-and-relabel into KNOB2 but anchors it on a calibrated s_cal and pairs it with the gate.

(5) Affordance / grasp-validity and physics checking in sim -- Dex-Net / Mahler et al. [23], and sim-rollout / VLM validators. WHAT: validate whether a grasp or interaction is physically feasible [23]; sim rollouts or VLM judges can label task-validity. DELTA: these are typically a HARD EXTERNAL VALIDATOR -- a binary gate run outside the loss -- not a CALIBRATED FREE FIELD feeding a per-sample BC loss with an exact-collapse identity. CONFER folds such a check in as ONE estimator channel (e2) and uses the labeled validator only at the ORACLE rung (R5). The validator becomes a component and a ceiling, not the method.

THE TWO BARRIERS THAT KEPT IT UNSOLVED. (a) It LOOKED like it needed a per-augmentation action label: knowing whether the reused action still works appears to require an expensive sim rollout or human/VLM judgment for every augmented sample -- prohibitive at the line's 250x augmentation scale [1]. The UNLOCK is that per-augmentation consistency can be estimated FOR FREE from existing signals (e1, e2, e3), with only a tiny held-out probe to fit c(.); no per-sample train-time label is required. (b) Selection and relabeling were studied SEPARATELY and never UNIFIED under one measured field: the selection community (R3) and the soft-label community (R4) developed their single knobs in isolation, on generic label noise, with no shared quantity and no safe default. CONFER places BOTH knobs on the SAME calibrated field s_cal and adds the EXACT-COLLAPSE recovery of keep-all (R2) that neither line needed, because neither had named s.

ONE CLEAN NOVELTY SENTENCE. CONFER is the first method to model the reused action label's consistency as a calibrated, free-to-estimate per-(demonstration, augmentation) field s(d -> a) and to act on it with BOTH a selection gate (gamma) and a soft-relabel reweighting (phi, lambda), while provably collapsing to vanilla keep-all hard-label augmentation at the identity default (E_collapse <= 1e-6).

STRONGEST PRIOR-ART RISK (flagged honestly). The most dangerous neighbor is family (5): grasp/physics validators (e.g., Dex-Net [23]) and confidence-filtered selective augmentation. A reviewer could argue that "filter out augmentations whose grasp looks infeasible" is already standard practice and that CONFER repackages it. CONFER's defensible deltas are three and concrete: (i) CALIBRATED FREE FIELD -- s is a continuous, probe-calibrated, per-sample quantity (reported with AUROC/ECE vs an oracle [19]) from free signals, not a hard external binary validator; (ii) TWO-KNOB UNIFICATION -- the SAME field drives BOTH a selection gate g_i AND a soft-relabel tilde_a_i, whereas prior validators are gate-only and merely drop data (the PLACEBO rung guards against "gains were just dropping data"); (iii) EXACT COLLAPSE -- a CI-gateable strict generalization back to keep-all with E_collapse <= 1e-6, which no external-validator pipeline provides. The novelty is not "check validity" -- it is MEASURING validity as a calibrated field and ACTING on it with two unified knobs under a provable safety net.

**[Question 5] - What are the key components of my approach and results?**

METHOD PIPELINE (end-to-end). Step 0 Generate: any generative augmenter (CopyPaste of segmented LVIS objects [11, 12] / inpaint / diffusion [15]) turns demo d into imagined scenes a, producing samples (o_i, a_i^demo). Step 1 Estimate s_hat_i for free from e1 policy self-agreement (closeness of a_i^pred to a_i^demo on o_i, computed with a circularity-safe FROZEN or CROSS-FIT teacher, never the live theta), e2 affordance/contact/grasp-pose+scale/support proxy from the segmentation + geometry the augmenter runs, and e3 multi-view consistency across the MT-ACT frames with per-view reliability weighting (O5). Step 2 Calibrate: s_cal_i = c(s_hat_i), with c(.) fit on a small held-out probe (sim-rollout success or VLM/human judge) via isotonic/Platt, anchored c(1) = 1. Step 3 KNOB1 gate: g_i = 1[s_cal_i >= gamma] routes which scenes enter the training buffer. Step 4 KNOB2 reweight + soft-relabel: w_i = phi(s_cal_i) (monotone non-decreasing, phi(1) = 1) and tilde_a_i = lambda(s_cal_i) * a_i^demo + (1 - lambda(s_cal_i)) * a_i^pred (lambda monotone non-decreasing, lambda(1) = 1, default conservative; a_i^pred from the same safe teacher). Step 5 Train pi_theta on L_CONFER = sum_i g_i * w_i * ell(pi_theta(o_i), tilde_a_i). Safety rails are folded in: circularity-safe teacher (O1/O7), do-no-harm non-inferiority gate (epsilon), and the EXACT-COLLAPSE check (disable estimation, set s_hat_i = 1 for all i => L_CONFER == L_base, E_collapse <= 1e-6).

DATASETS / BENCHMARKS (all real). RLBench (multi-task manipulation); Meta-World (multi-task pick-place); CALVIN (long-horizon language-conditioned manipulation); the Transporter / Ravens pick-place suite [5]; a real RoboAgent-style pick-place suite (real-robot eval); and the paper's own MT-AUG setup INCLUDING the plasticity / data-reusability protocol (38 tasks -> + place-toast-in-toaster-oven new task; 50 traj x 4 aug/frame = 250 augmented samples per frame budget) [1]. Evaluation axes (the paper's): Unseen Env / Unseen place / Unseen pick, at 1 / 10 / 100 demos.

BACKBONES (field is backbone-agnostic): MT-ACT / ACT [7], TransporterNet [5], CLIPort [6], Diffusion Policy [8].

BASELINES = boundary recoveries R1..R5 + a strong competitor. R1 No-Aug; R2 vanilla keep-all semantic aug (CopyPaste / MT-AUG; GenAug / ROSIE / CACTI hard-label reuse) -- the named status quo and COLLAPSE TARGET; R3 pure data-selection / confidence-filter (KNOB2 off); R4 pure soft-label / reweight BC (KNOB1 off); R5 oracle filter (sim/VLM-labeled validity, the ceiling); PLUS a strong generative-augmentation competitor (diffusion/inpaint keep-all) to show CONFER is additive on top of any augmenter.

METRICS. Task success rate, POOLED and STRATIFIED by unseen-object / unseen-place / unseen-env x augmentation strength (stratified is PRIMARY); calibration of the field, AUROC and ECE of s_cal vs oracle preservation labels (and raw s_hat vs calibrated, to show the lift); keep_rate, reported with every success number (rate-matched placebo); compute overhead vs baseline (to evidence the field is estimated for free/low-overhead); and E_collapse (the exact-collapse budget, CI-gateable, required <= 1e-6).

ABLATION GRID. Orthogonal 2x2 {KNOB1 on/off} x {KNOB2 on/off} (recovers R2, R3, R4, and full CONFER at the four corners); estimator channels {e1, e2, e3, and all combos} x {raw s_hat vs calibrated c(.)}; gamma sweep (endpoints recover R1 gate-all-out and R2 gate-all-in); augmentation-strength sweep (low -> high); lambda soft-relabel sweep (conservative -> aggressive); teacher source {in-loop theta, frozen snapshot, cross-fit} to expose circularity (O1).

FALSIFIER-LADDER RUNS (explicit PASS conditions). ORACLE (ceiling, R5): replace s_hat_i with true labeled consistency -- PASS if baseline < CONFER <= oracle AND the oracle shows real headroom; FALSIFIES (estimator) if free s_hat is no better than PLACEBO. PLACEBO (rate-matched consistent-shuffle): random field at the SAME keep_rate -- PASS if CONFER > placebo by more than seed noise at matched keep_rate; FALSIFIES if the gain was merely dropping data. RECOVERY (reproduces baseline): disable estimation (set s_hat_i = 1) -- PASS if L_CONFER == L_base sample-for-sample, E_collapse <= 1e-6, reproducing R2 to seed; FALSIFIES (construction) on any deviation > 1e-6. LEAKAGE: probe set for c(.) disjoint from eval, s_hat never reads eval labels, calibration split frozen -- PASS if gains survive the strict disjoint split; FALSIFIES if gains vanish when leakage is removed. DO-NO-HARM: constant-validity / unseen-environment-only regime -- PASS if pooled success >= baseline - epsilon on EVERY cell; FALSIFIES on any regression beyond epsilon at the identity/near-identity default.

EXPECTED OUTCOMES (as HYPOTHESES; provable items flagged). H1 [conjectured->measured]: a MODEST pooled lift over keep-all R2 but a LARGE lift at unseen objects / unseen places and at high augmentation strength -- the GRADIENT of lift across strata is itself the evidence that the field measures heterogeneity (MIRAGE is real). H2 [conjectured->measured]: both knobs contribute -- KNOB1 removes outright noise, KNOB2 recovers borderline mass a hard gate would waste; full CONFER > R3 and > R4 individually. H3 [conjectured->measured]: e2 (affordance/contact/geometry) is the strongest single estimator channel; e1 alone is weakest/most circularity-prone; e1+e2+e3 calibrated is best. H4 [conjectured->measured]: calibrated s_cal achieves AUROC well above 0.5 against oracle preservation labels, with ECE far below raw confidence (calibration is load-bearing). H5 [conjectured->measured]: a cross-fit / frozen teacher beats an in-loop teacher -- direct confirmation that circularity (O1) is real, not a strawman. H6 [conjectured->measured]: free s_hat approaches the ORACLE (R5) ceiling and clearly beats the rate-matched PLACEBO. P1 [provable-by-construction]: EXACT-COLLAPSE -- set s_hat_i = 1 for all i => L_CONFER == L_base, E_collapse <= 1e-6 (zero regression at identity). P2 [provable-by-construction]: boundary recoveries R1..R5 are exact special points of the field. P3 [provable-by-construction underwriting a conjectured->measured outcome]: DO-NO-HARM non-inferiority within epsilon at the identity/near-identity default, underwritten by P1; note P1 holds exactly under exact arithmetic, while empirical near-identity behavior under finite precision and approximate calibration is a separate, measured claim.

Responsible-AI and Deployment Considerations. (1) Decision-support framing: CONFER's three rails (KNOB1 gate, DO-NO-HARM epsilon, EXACT-COLLAPSE) are genuine structural mitigations but all assume s_cal is faithful, so CONFER is positioned as a DECISION-SUPPORT tool, not an autonomous safety gate -- the probe fit c(.) and a human spot-check of high-s_cal augmented samples are required before any physical-hardware deployment, especially for high-force or unstructured tasks. (2) Confidently-wrong s_cal residual risk: a c(.) fit on easy sim scenes can output s_cal that is systematically too high on novel-object distribution-shift cases (where MIRAGE is most severe), so we recommend a conservative (high) gamma on first real-world runs, widening only after calibration transfer is confirmed, and report ECE of s_cal separately on the sim-probe subset and on a held-out real subset. (3) Probe governance: VLM judges are treated as a NOISY WEAK SUPERVISOR (they under-report geometrically impossible-but-plausible grasps), so we report VLM-vs-human inter-annotator agreement, require a minimum human-validated probe split (suggested >= 100-200 examples) for any c(.) that drives real-robot training, and document the VLM version and prompt template. (4) Sim-to-real transfer: refit c(.) on a small real labeled subset (suggested >= 50 labeled real outcomes per object category), report calibration transfer as AUROC/ECE on real labels separately from sim, and NEVER pool sim and real numbers. (5) Data/model licensing: LVIS/COCO instances [12] restrict commercial deployment, Stable Diffusion [15] imagery inherits CreativeML Open RAIL-M use restrictions (and may reproduce memorized content), so we list every dataset/model license in a dedicated assets section and release the PROBE set (with labels and rollout outcomes) so c(.) is independently verifiable. (6) Evaluation honesty: keep_rate is a REQUIRED (not optional) reporting item alongside every success number, all expected outcomes H1-H6 are explicitly HYPOTHESES (the provable EXACT-COLLAPSE identity does not imply empirical non-inferiority), and generalization claims are scoped strictly to the tested tasks, objects, and environments -- novel embodiments, high-force tasks, and densely cluttered scenes are outside the validated scope.
